mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and consumer income growth, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2012 payroll growth of 133,300 thousand persons indicates ongoing labor market recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth months produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate below 0.25% signals an extended zero lower bound regime where monetary policy is maximally accommodative, reducing equity risk premiums and supporting equity valuations through suppressed discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2012 federal funds rate at 0.13% confirms ZLB regime active
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during ZLB periods (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) indicates strengthening consumer and residential demand, which signals improving economic momentum and corporate profit expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when both indicators show month-over-month growth.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive month-over-month growth
evidence_refs: fact:July 2012 housing starts at 746,000 units and retail sales at 406,748 million dollars
regime_hint: demand-led expansion with synchronized consumer and residential strength
failure_condition: Instances where both RSAFS and HOUST show positive MoM growth produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential demand destruction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2012 industrial production at 96.82 index points
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO declines > 0.2% produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO changes and forward returns exceeds -0.02 (i.e., signal direction reverses).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 8.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which dampens discretionary spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2012 unemployment rate at 8.1% indicates elevated joblessness
regime_hint: elevated unemployment and labor market slack regime
failure_condition: Periods with UNRATE > 8.0% produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE and forward returns falls below -0.02 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with accommodative federal funds rate below 0.50% creates a real negative interest rate environment that erodes bond returns and redirects capital flows toward equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2012 CPI at 230.10 index and federal funds at 0.13% creates negative real rate environment
regime_hint: financial repression regime with negative real rates
failure_condition: Instances meeting both trigger conditions produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of cases, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows acceleration above 2.0% annualized rate, signaling robust economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2012 real GDP at 13,564.5 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.0% produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining more than 10% month-over-month signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and forward-looking construction demand, which predicts weakness in durable goods orders and employment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2012 housing starts at 746,000 units provide baseline for detecting sharp contractions
regime_hint: demand shock or housing cycle downturn
failure_condition: Months with HOUST declines > 10% produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC between HOUST changes and forward returns exceeds -0.02 in absolute value.
